commands.review_summary:
The branch adds pre-normalization runtime tool-schema quarantine for agent attempts and compaction, preserves raw active tool names for bundled MCP/LSP reservations, and adds focused tests with synthetic fixtures.

PR surface: Source +155, Tests +416. Total +571 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main normalizes raw active tool schemas before quarantine, and a circular schema can enter recursive normalization before the diagnostic omission path runs.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer compatibility acceptance for the changed invalid-tool failure mode, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid runtime tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

Quarantine active runtime tools whose schemas cannot safely reach provider/runtime schema projection, including assistant-turn startup and compaction.

This now covers the earlier tool-parameter normalization boundary too: schemas that are not JSON-serializable are left untouched by schema normalization so the runtime quarantine path can omit and report them instead of crashing before diagnostics.

Healthy sibling tools continue. Quarantined active tool names remain reserved for bundled MCP/LSP materialization, so a bundled tool cannot silently reuse the name of an omitted active tool.

The test fixtures use neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names.

## Verification

- Rebased onto current `origin/main` `c8f7e9102b88`; pushed head `b5b9c0452d2f`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts`: passed 2 shards / 169 tests after the final rebase.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...`: passed for the touched schema/quarantine/attempt/compaction files.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`: passed for the touched schema/quarantine/attempt/compaction files.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` and `git diff --check`: passed.
- Added-line scrub for the originally reported fixture name, private paths, and secret-looking strings: clean.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings; overall patch correct.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: passed on run `run_7403d1c41a29`, lease `cbx_26569933db9f` / `blue-crab`, provider `aws`, command 8m50s, total 11m27s, lanes `core` and `coreTests`, lease stopped=true.
- After that gate, `origin/main` moved with unrelated docs/agent/exec/plugin-state changes; rebase onto `c8f7e9102b88` was clean and focused Vitest still passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A single active tool with a schema that cannot survive provider/runtime schema projection is quarantined before assistant session creation instead of crashing the whole assistant turn.

Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest/format/lint proof through repo wrappers; remote Linux changed gate through AWS Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Verification above.

Evidence after fix: Focused runner coverage shows session creation receives only healthy sibling tools when an active tool has an unsupported or non-serializable schema. Schema-normalizer coverage verifies non-JSON-serializable schemas are left for runtime quarantine instead of crashing early. Attempt and compaction coverage verifies quarantined active names remain reserved for bundled MCP/LSP runtimes.

Observed result after fix: Healthy tools continue; active bad tools are reported through the existing trusted diagnostic/quarantine path; bundled tools cannot take over quarantined active names.

What was not tested: Live provider calls.
target.author: vincentkoc
